title: PHYTOMap in Arabidopsis root tips
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3bkn8","text":"Retrieving the complex responses of individual cells in the native three-dimensional tissue context is crucial for a complete understanding of tissue functions. Here, we present PHYTOMap (Plant HYbridization-based Targeted Observation of gene expression Map), a multiplexed fluorescencein situhybridization method that enables single-cell and spatial analysis of gene expression in whole-mount plant tissue in a transgene-free manner and at low cost. We applied PHYTOMap to simultaneously analyze 28 cell type marker genes inArabidopsisroots and successfully identified major cell types, demonstrating that our method can substantially accelerate the spatial mapping of marker genes defined in single-cell RNA-seq datasets in complex plant tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":286,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":525,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"38mfn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"l3sel","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"b2pbo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ds70","text":"[Reagents]","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3au9r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cqjg","text":"DPBST","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5qkr0","text":"0.1% (vol/vol) Tween-20 in 1x DPBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3i32u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"44nio","text":"DPBSTR","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9riib","text":"DPBST + 1:100 SUPERaseIN","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9smia","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dbp97","text":"Cell wall digestion enzyme solution (CWDES; 10x stock)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":54}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1fn0c","text":"250 mg macerozyme, 250 mg cellulase, 500 mg pectinase in 50 mL Nuclease-free water. Filter sterilize (0.22 μm filter) and store aliquots of 1 mL at -20°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1h37b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7h49g","text":"Proteinase K buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dusoi","text":"ABReagentAmount10x equiPhi29 DNA polymerase buffer10 µL10 mM dNTP2.5 µL4 mM aminoallyl-dUTP0.5 µLBSA (20 mg/ml)0.5 µLDTT (100 mM)1 µLSUPERase IN1 µLNuclease Free Water 84.5 µL","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":175}],"data":{}},{"key":"er1h4","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13ek4","text":"FAA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2h7j5","text":"ABReagentAmount1M Tris-HCl (pH 8.0)5 mL0.5 M EDTA (pH 8.0)5 mLNuclease Free Water up to 50 mL","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":93}],"data":{}},{"key":"5e7p9","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ae21h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e440d","text":" ","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"93j1k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"64879B4A341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":337,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":[{\"key\":\"fiehm\",\"text\":\"PHYTOMap principles. a, Target mRNA molecules are hybridized by DNA probes (SNAIL probes) that harbor mRNA species-specific barcode sequences (pink bars). The barcode-containing DNA probes are circularized by ligation and amplified in situ by rolling circle amplification (RCA). b, Amplified DNA barcodes are detected by sequence-by-hybridization. Different fluorescent probes target four DNA barcodes for each imaging round. After the imaging, fluorescent probes are stripped away, and another set of four genes is targeted.\",\"type\":\"unstyled\",\"depth\":0,\"inlineStyleRanges\":[{\"style\":\"bold\",\"offset\":0,\"length\":19},{\"style\":\"italic\",\"offset\":232,\"length\":7}],\"entityRanges\":[],\"data\":{}}],\"entityMap\":{}}","mime":"image/png","nestedSelector":null,"original_name":"fig1.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/mqrebhvaf.jpg","shadow":false,"source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/mqrcbhvaf.jpg","webp_placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/mqrdbhvaf.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/mqrbbhvaf.webp","width":550}},"1":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":"","7_0":"","7_1":"","8_0":"","8_1":"","A_1":{"className":"_dt-bold"},"B_1":{"className":"_dt-bold"}},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":["161",100],"data":[["Reagent","Amount"],["1M steps: Cut Arabidopsis root tips (approximately 1 cm) with razor blade and place them on a Poly-D-Lysine coated dish. The tissue should adhere to the dish well.
Rehydrate the tissue by 00:05:00 successive washes with 100 µL 75%, 50%, 25% MeOH in DPBST.
Mix gene specific probes at the concentration of 5 nM per oligo.
Wash the tissue with 100 µL DPBSTR for 00:05:00 .
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL 2xSSC for 00:01:00 .

Incubate the tissue in 100 µL of FAA (see MATERIALS) at Room temperature for 01:00:00 . If most of the root tips detach from the dish, it is necessary to optimize the mounting process for the samples.
Wash the tissue successively for 00:10:00 at Room temperature with 100 µL 70% EtOH, 90% EtOH, and twice with 100% EtOH.
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL 100% MeOH for 00:10:00 and leave the tissue in MeOH after the second wash at -20 °C Overnight .
Remove the CWDES and add 100 µL fresh & cold 1x CWDES. Then, incubate the tissue at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL DPBSTR (see MATERIALS).
Fix the tissue by incubating in 100 µL 10% (v/v) formaldehyde in DPBST at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL DPBSTR at Room temperature ( 00:01:00 each).
Incubate the tissue in 100 µL digestion solution at 37 °C for 00:30:00 . A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 Proteinase K buffer (see MATERIALS) 99 µL 3 Proteinase K 1 µL Digestion solution
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL DPBSTR at Room temperature ( 00:01:00 each).
Fix the tissue by incubating in 100 µL 10% (v/v) formaldehyde in DPBST at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL DPBSTR at Room temperature ( 00:05:00 each).
Heat the probe mixture at 90 °C for 00:03:00 and let it cool down at Room temperature .

Incubate the tissue in the hybridization mixture at 40 °C for 03:00:00 or Overnight %s %s ABReagentAmount20xSSC10 µLFormamide30 µL10% Triton-X10 µL200 mM RVC10 µLSUPERase IN1 µLProbe mix (500 nM per oligo)2 µLNuclease Free Water 37 µL The design of probes is described in our paper .
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL DPBSTR at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .

Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL 4xSSC in DPBSTR at 37 °C for 00:30:00 . %s %s ABReagentAmount20xSSC10 µLFormamide30 µL10% Triton-X10 µL200 mM RVC10 µLSUPERase IN1 µLProbe mix (500 nM per oligo)2 µLNuclease Free Water 37 µL
Rinse the tissue with 100 µL DPBSTR at Room temperature .

Incubate the tissue in 100 µL ligation mixture WITHOUT ligase On ice for 00:05:00 . A B 1 Reagent Amount 2 10x ligation buffer 10 µL 3 BSA (2mg/ml) 0.5 µL 4 SUPERase IN 1 µL 5 Nuclease Free Water 88.5 µL Ligation mixture without ligase
Incubate the tissue in 100 µL ligation mixture WITH ligase at Room temperature Overnight . %s %s ABReagentAmount10x ligation buffer10 µLBSA (2mg/ml)0.5 µLSUPERase IN1 µLNuclease Free Water 88.5 µL

Incubate the tissue in 100 µL RCA mixture WITHOUT equiPhi29 DNA polymerase On ice for 00:05:00 . %s %s ABReagentAmount10x ligation buffer10 µLBSA (2mg/ml)0.5 µLSUPERase IN1 µLNuclease Free Water 88.5 µL

Incubate the tissue in 100 µL RCA mixture WITH equiPhi29 DNA polymerase at 37 °C Overnight . %s %s ABReagentAmount10x equiPhi29 DNA polymerase buffer10 µL10 mM dNTP2.5 µL4 mM aminoallyl-dUTP0.5 µLBSA (20 mg/ml)0.5 µLDTT (100 mM)1 µLSUPERase IN1 µLNuclease Free Water 84.5 µL
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL DPBSTR for 00:10:00 .

Incubate the tissue in 100 µL BS(PEG9) solution at Room temperature for 01:00:00 . %s %s ABReagentAmount10x equiPhi29 DNA polymerase buffer10 µL10 mM dNTP2.5 µL4 mM aminoallyl-dUTP0.5 µLBSA (20 mg/ml)0.5 µLDTT (100 mM)1 µLSUPERase IN1 µLequiPhi29 DNA polymerase5 µLNuclease Free Water 79.5 µL BS(PEG9) stock is made by adding 465 µL DMSO into a vial of 100 mg BS(PEG9) and is stored desiccated at -20ºC.
Aspire BS(PEG9) solution and incubate the tissue in 100 µL 1 M Tris-HCl pH 8.0 at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .

Rinse the tissue in 100 µL DPBST. If multiple rounds of imaging are not necessary, gel embedding may be skipped. However, it is important to note that without gel embedding, there is a higher risk of tissue detachment or movement between imaging rounds. Therefore, it is recommended to carefully handle the tissue.
Incubate the tissue in 100 µL monomer solution On ice for 00:30:00 . %s %s ABReagentAmountDPBST196 µLBS(PEG9) stock4 µL

Aspire the monomer solution and add 50 µL gelling solution. Place a Gel Slick-coated glass coverslip on top of the tissue, and carefully aspirate any excess gelling solution. Incubate the tissue at Room temperature for 01:00:00 –– 02:00:00 until the gel solidifies. %s %s ABReagentAmount20% acrylamide20 µL2% bis-acrylamide10 µL2xSSC70 µL Coating a coverslip with Gel Slick: Add Gel Slick Solution onto the coverslip, then wipe gently with a Kimwipe to spread the Gel Slick Solution.
Carefully remove the coverslip with forceps, and wash the tissue with 100 µL DPBST for 00:05:00 .
Incubate the tissue in ClearSee until imaging.
Incubate the tissue in 100 µL Bridge probe mixture at Room temperature for 01:00:00 . %s %s ABReagentAmountMonomer solution48.9 µL10% APS stock1 µLTEMED0.1 µL
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL 2xSSC for 00:01:00 .

Incubate the tissue in 100 µL Detection probe mixture at Room temperature for 01:00:00 . %s %s ABReagentAmount2x hybridization buffer50 µLBridge probes (10uM stock) 1 µL each (typically 4 probes)Nuclease free water up to 100 µL
Wash the tissue twice with 100 µL 2xSSC for 00:01:00 .
Wash the tissue with 100 µL ClearSee, and keep it in ClearSee for imaging.
Imaging with a confocal microscope.

After the imaging, strip the bridge/detection probes by incubating the tissue in 100 µL stripping solution (65% formamide in 2xSSC) at 30 °C for 00:30:00 . %s %s ABReagentAmount2x hybridization buffer50 µLDetection probes (10uM stock) 1 µL each (4 probes)Calcofluor White1 µLNuclease free water up to 100 µL
Go to Step 35 for the next round of imaging.
Incubate the tissues in 100 µL 1x CWDES (see MATERIALS) On ice for 00:05:00 . %s %s ABReagentAmount10x CWDES10 µLSUPERase IN1 µLDPBST89 µL
